service: MS2 Django AI — GCP Cloud Run + AWS DynamoDB

visualSearch.honestMethod: Classical computer-vision baseline — NOT a neural network / deep learning. Perceptual hash (pHash, 64 dims) + HSV color histogram (16 bins x 3 channels = 48 dims) => 112-dim L2-normalized vector, compared by cosine similarity. Deterministic, fast, no GPU. A documented interface seam (encode_image) allows swapping in a CLIP/ResNet backbone later.
visualSearch.libraries: imagehash==4.3.1, Pillow==10.4.0, numpy==1.26.4 (no torch / no tensorflow / no pretrained weights)
visualSearch.syncProof: POST /api/v1/ai/catalog/sync/ -> stored 3 embeddings (DEMO-RED, DEMO-BLUE, DEMO-GREEN), 112-dim each, in DynamoDB table ficct_product_embeddings (errors: [])

searchProof.interpretation: The exact-match image ranks 1.0 and differently-colored items rank lower via the HSV histogram — ranking is meaningful and correct, proving the end-to-end pipeline (encode query -> cosine vs stored embeddings -> ranked matches + scores).

forecasting.method: Holt linear (double exponential smoothing), pure numpy
forecasting.proven: executed live from the Angular AI page (forecast computed)

clustering.method: KMeans (scikit-learn 1.5.1) on RFM features; segments persisted to DynamoDB ficct_customer_segments
clustering.proven: executed live from the Angular AI page (segments returned)

auth: RS256 JWT (issuer ficct-go, audience ficct-django); IsAuthenticated; clustering/forecasting require admin/staff
